patch.value: Promote governance, validation, health, and maintenance primitives to the public SDK: validate, health, gc, changelog/reporting hooks

Typed SDK access to the governance engine (metadata profiles, resolution checks, lifecycle rules, file-link validation, history-drift), health checks, and gc/maintenance so integrators can enforce the same quality bars programmatically. Duplicate check: no existing governance-primitive SDK item.

SDK exports typed validate/health/gc primitives returning the same structured check results the CLI prints; ops commands consume only these primitives; docstrings + linked tests + SDK reference docs; zero behavior change

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Audit #15 scope note: decide telemetry-queue/runtime primitives here — either promote them with validate/health/gc (they share the ops noun per pm-6apl) or explicitly document them as CLI-only in the SDK reference. The SDK domain matrix should leave no command family unmapped.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Promotion slicing S1-S4 (pass #53 code survey). Territory = ~5.1k lines of CLI-side domain logic: validate.ts 2623 + health.ts 2161 + gc.ts 341; SDK index exports NOTHING for validate/health/gc today. Both blockers (pm-lodl, pm-muhw) closed - actionable now.
S1 validate check-engine (~150m): check orchestration lives in cli/commands/validate.ts - it imports core scanners (scanHistoryDrift, findDuplicateIssueCodes, buildMissingByTypeCounts, core/validate/* helpers) but assembles checks/counts/worklists inside the command. Extract a typed check-producer registry into core/validate/ and export runValidateChecks() returning the same structured envelope (checks[].status, *_count scalars, details.*_item_ids); validate.ts shrinks to flag-parse + render.
S2 health probe registry (~120m): same move for health.ts - probes (store layout, scanLockHealth, vectorization staleness via collectStaleVectorizationIds, extension registrations, history drift + stream policy) become typed producers; export runHealthProbes(). Natural fix site for GH-457/pm-tu71 - promotion adds a per-probe timeout surface.
S3 gc primitive (~45m): gc.ts is small (341 lines, 4 fns); export typed gc plan/apply primitive; CLI consumes it.
S4 reporting/remediation hooks (~60m): expose buildRemediationCommands (core/diagnostics/remediation.ts) through the SDK; type the changelog/reporting hooks; docstrings + linked tests + SDK reference docs for S1-S3 exports.
Zero-behavior-change gate: validate/health/gc CLI JSON output byte-identical, existing specs green. Recommended entry: S1 (largest file, self-contained).

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Review adjudication 2026-07-15: accepted actionable author warnings as blocking overall health, complete CommandLineToArgvW quote/backslash escaping, and junction-compatible Windows copy tests. Rejected the relative-destination crash report because copyExtensionDirectoryWithoutSelfNesting resolves the destination with path.resolve before parsing its root. Defensive legacy-payload guards do not apply to private helpers fed directly by the same-process typed scanner; full-stream performance suggestions describe pre-existing opt-in integrity/compaction behavior and remain under the scale roadmap rather than changing this compatibility move. Focused 195 tests, typecheck, formatting, and strict quality gates pass.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Greptile pre-push review evidence (2026-07-15): accepted P1 finding comment-1784108230323-vfmohmi. Missing or invalid timestamps now classify unknown-author history as legacy informational data; only parseable post-baseline events are actionable. Added explicit missing/invalid timestamp regression coverage. Focused author-attribution and health suites pass (59 tests), typecheck passes, and diff check is clean.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Hosted analyzer correction (2026-07-15): accepted GitHub code-scanning alert 26 (CodeQL polynomial-regex risk) and the renewed DeepSource classifier-complexity finding on PR #568 head 092de7ae. Windows CommandLineToArgvW rendering now uses a linear character scanner with explicit pending-backslash accounting; author attribution uses a typed unknown-author/post-baseline policy matrix, keeping missing or invalid timestamps legacy without control-flow complexity. Focused 66 tests, typecheck, strict static/docstring/package/token gates, and full 291-file/5,721-test coverage pass at exact 100/100/100/100 (37,023 statements, 26,530 branches, 7,463 functions, 36,138 lines).

value.text: DeepSource structural correction (2026-07-15): the current-head dashboard exposed 15 diff findings that GitHub inline comments omitted—complexity in quoteCommandArg, extension policy normalization, copy safety, and install locks plus global declarations/docstrings. Windows quoting is now split into a public linear primitive; copy-safety and owner-bound lock orchestration moved into src/sdk/extension/install-runtime.ts with typed reusable APIs; extension policy defaults use normalized object composition. The monolith shrank and strict file-length/static/docstring/package/token gates pass. Typecheck, 143 focused tests, and full 291-file/5,721-test coverage pass at exact 100/100/100/100 (37,038 statements, 26,514 branches, 7,467 functions, 36,153 lines).

value.author: codex-root
value.text: DeepSource follow-up batch (2026-07-15): the prior 15 findings were cleared; the next capped report exposed the analyzer's invalid global-scope rule across ESM helpers plus lifecycle suggestion complexity and one use-before-definition ordering issue. Converted all 50 top-level extension helpers to module-scoped arrow constants, flattened lifecycle target ranking into a deterministic distance pipeline, and ordered copy primitives before consumers. Typecheck, strict static/docstring/file-length/package/token gates, 136 focused tests, and full 291-file/5,721-test coverage pass at exact 100/100/100/100 (37,075 statements, 26,504 branches, 7,469 functions, 36,190 lines).

Verification: pnpm typecheck passed; tests/unit/extensions/extension-command.spec.ts passed 124/124; pnpm quality:static passed including 100% docstrings/package-boundary/token gates; full coverage passed 291 files and 5721 tests at 100/100/100/100 (37064 statements, 26496 branches, 7476 functions, 36179 lines).

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Hosted review round 2026-07-15: accepted four current-head CodeRabbit findings. The extension SDK now bounds remote update work and git timeouts, compares annotated tags against peeled commits, renders scaffold commands without shell substitution exposure, rejects unsafe lock leases, serializes all shared extension-state mutations, and rolls back files plus metadata after partial install persistence failures. Focused extension suite passes 133/133 and typecheck is clean; full gates pending.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Verification evidence 2026-07-15: review-driven extension lifecycle hardening passes pnpm typecheck, focused extension tests (133/133), pnpm quality:static (including 100% docstring coverage), full sandbox coverage (5730 tests; 100/100/100/100), packed-package smoke, the 88-command package-first dogfood matrix, pm health, pm validate with zero history drift, deep package doctor, and two isolated temporary-directory installed-package workflows including declarative scaffold/install/runtime registration and strict item lifecycle validation. The release-gate Greptile stage correctly refused to review eight uncommitted files; the committed-head Greptile review remains required before push. Live Sentry/telemetry gate is healthy: zero blocking critical/high issues in 14 days, 0.33% finish-error rate, and zero failures missing error codes.

value.author: codex-root

Accepted its sole minor style observation by moving the telemetry source-context constant below the complete import block in the promoted health SDK module; focused verification and a final committed-head review follow before push.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Hosted review round on head 13ecf76f: accepted Greptile P1 npx -c/--call reference parsing; CodeRabbit transactional rollback, concurrency validation, and Error.message findings; and Gemini legacy payload plus corrupt stale-lock findings. Install snapshot/restore is now a focused SDK module, rollback failures preserve both error contexts, stale corrupt locks recover, bounded mapping rejects invalid limits, and public health projections retain legacy attribution semantics. Verification passes 188 focused tests, typecheck, strict static/docstring/package/token gates, and the complete 291-file/5732-test suite at exact 100/100/100/100 coverage.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Review evidence: exact-head hosted round completed with Greptile green and 29 hosted successes. DeepScan/DeepSource/static surfaced the same two strict findings: incorrect AggregateError cause and linked-command parser cognitive complexity. Fixed both by preserving rollbackError as the direct cause while retaining both errors in AggregateError.errors, and simplifying npx option advancement without a one-use wrapper. Focused extension/linked-command regressions pass (136/136), focused ESLint is clean, and the full static quality/package-boundary/token-budget gate passes.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Review evidence: final hosted round accepted CodeRabbit stale-lock read-safety finding. Owner metadata reads now distinguish missing or malformed state from real filesystem failures; EACCES and similar failures propagate without deleting the lock, while absent/corrupt stale owners remain recoverable. Added a protected-lock regression; focused extension suite passes 134/134 and focused ESLint is clean. Greptile multi-positional finding was rejected against live contracts because all referenced command families accept exactly one non-variadic item id; arbitrary later positionals are payload text, and multiple valid shell invocations are already segmented.
